title: ARPA-H PROSPR Program Awards $144M for First Large-Scale Human Aging Trials
description: The U.S. government's ARPA-H agency awarded $144M to seven research teams under its PROSPR program to conduct the first large-scale clinical trials targeting aging directly. UT Health San Antonio will test rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin in 700+ adults, while Brown University will test an HIV drug (Censavudine) to suppress retrotransposons linked to age-related inflammation. This marks a turning point where aging is treated as a targetable condition, not just a background risk factor.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences Reports Positive Phase 1 Results for First-Ever GPX4 Senolytic Drug
description: Rubedo Life Sciences announced positive Phase 1 results for RLS-1496, the first GPX4 modulator tested in humans for cellular rejuvenation. The topical senolytic showed dose-dependent reduction in senescent cells, a 20% reduction in epidermal thickness, and statistically significant improvement in psoriasis severity with no serious adverse events. This is a milestone for the senolytics field, demonstrating that selective senescent cell clearance is achievable and tolerable in humans.

title: NIA Interventions Testing Program March 2026 Results: 11 Compounds Fail to Extend Lifespan
description: The NIA's Interventions Testing Program released its March 2026 batch, testing 11 compounds across 30,000+ mice at three independent sites. None extended lifespan. Alpha-ketoglutarate, astaxanthin, meclizine, atorvastatin+telmisartan combo, and others all showed zero benefit. Pioglitazone shortened female lifespan by 6.2%. After 20 years and 80+ compounds tested, only rapamycin, acarbose, canagliflozin, and 17-alpha estradiol remain validated -- all cheap generics.

title: China's Landmark Stem Cell and Gene Editing Regulation Takes Effect May 1, 2026
description: China's comprehensive regulation on stem cells and gene editing technologies, signed by Premier Li Qiang, takes effect May 1, 2026. It establishes a unified framework for clinical research and application of iPSCs, CAR-T, CRISPR, and gene therapies. Only Class-A tertiary hospitals with full ethics review can conduct studies. China has simultaneously led formulation of four international stem cell standards, positioning itself at the forefront of regenerative medicine translation.

title: Longevity Center Europe: DunedinPACE Data Shows Clients Aging 22% Slower Than Average
description: Longevity Center Europe published 2025 DunedinPACE results from 80+ clients, showing an average biological aging pace of 0.78 -- meaning they age 22% slower than the general population. At least 10 clients achieved a pace below Bryan Johnson's reported 0.65. This data suggests that structured longevity protocols built around lifestyle optimization, sleep, stress management, and medical interventions can meaningfully slow biological aging at scale.

title: ARPA-H Awards $144M for First Clinical Trials Targeting Aging Directly
description: ARPA-H's PROSPR program awarded $144M to seven teams for the first phase 3 trials of aging interventions, aiming to validate biomarkers and test rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Major Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and Dapagliflozin Aging Trial
description: Over 700 South Texans will participate in a landmark UT Health study testing three drugs that might slow biological aging in older adults.

title: UT Health Launches NIA-Funded Multi-Phase Rapamycin Clinical Study
description: UT Health San Antonio launched an NIA-funded clinical study with 84 older adults to evaluate rapamycin's dosing, safety, and biological effects on healthy aging.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences Reports Positive Phase 1 Results for Senolytic Drug RLS-1496
description: RLS-1496, the first GPX4 modulator tested in humans, showed dose-dependent senescent cell reduction, 20% epidermal thickness decrease, and clinical improvement in psoriasis and atopic dermatitis.

title: Brown University Receives $22M to Test HIV Drug Censavudine Against Biological Aging
description: ARPA-H awarded Brown up to $22M to test whether censavudine (TPN-101) can suppress retrotransposon activity and reduce biological aging in 200+ adults aged 60-65 over 48 weeks.

title: NIA Interventions Testing Program March 2026: 11 Compounds Tested, None Extended Lifespan
description: Alpha-ketoglutarate, astaxanthin, and 9 other compounds failed to extend mouse lifespan in the ITP's latest batch. After 80+ compounds, only rapamycin, acarbose, canagliflozin, and 17-alpha estradiol remain validated.

title: Cedars-Sinai: Senolytics Clear Up to 70% of Zombie Cells, New Senosensitizers Proposed
description: Preclinical study found senolytic drugs eliminate 30-70% of senescent cells. Remaining resistant cells can be targeted by a new drug class called senosensitizers.

title: Senolytics Pipeline 2026: Science Strong, Clinical Proof Still Emerging
description: A 2026 Nature Aging study compared 21 senolytic agents, finding ABT263 and ARV825 most effective. The field is splitting into three lanes: first-gen D+Q, localized precision, and next-gen discovery.

title: Life Biosciences Secures FDA Approval for First Human Cellular Reprogramming Trial
description: David Sinclair's Life Biosciences received FDA approval for a Phase 1 trial of partial epigenetic reprogramming to restore vision in glaucoma and NAION patients, marking a historic first.

title: Low-Dose Rapamycin Significantly Reduces Senescence Marker p21 in Human Immune Cells
description: Research by Kell, Jones, and colleagues found that low-dose rapamycin significantly reduced p21, a marker of cellular senescence, in immune cells from older adults.

title: Bryan Johnson Blueprint 2026 Update: Drops Rapamycin, Adds Lithium and NDGA
description: Bryan Johnson's updated 2026 supplement stack drops rapamycin, adds low-dose lithium and NDGA for brain function, and switches NMN/NR to six days per week.

title: Longevity Biotech Market Valued at $12.5B, Projected to Reach $45.8B by 2033
description: The global longevity biotech market is valued at $12.5B in 2026, projected to reach $45.8B by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 20.4%, driven by AI integration and clinical translation.

title: Loyal's Dog Longevity Drug LOY-002 Clears Second FDA Milestone
description: The FDA accepted the Target Animal Safety package for LOY-002, completing 2 of 3 technical sections needed for conditional approval of the first lifespan extension drug for senior dogs.

title: China Stem Cell and Gene Editing Regulation Takes Effect May 1, 2026
description: China's comprehensive regulation on stem cells, iPSCs, CAR-T, CRISPR, and gene therapies establishes a unified clinical framework. China also led four international stem cell standards.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising 2026: $13B+ in Venture Funding
description: Altos Labs leads at $3.0B, followed by Retro Biosciences ($1.2B). Top 10 startups capture 67% of all longevity funding. Corsera Health raised the largest Series A ($80M) for cardiovascular aging.

title: Anti-Aging Therapy Market Targets $420B Milestone
description: Investors are eyeing explosive growth in anti-aging therapies as the market targets a $420B milestone, driven by scalable healthtech and venture capital convergence.

title: The Business of Longevity: Turning Aging into an Investable Market
description: Biotech labs, healthtech startups, and VC firms are converging around the thesis that aging is a programmable, measurable, and investable frontier.

title: Longevity Biotech VC Funding Hits $2.1B in Therapeutics and Drug Discovery
description: In 2024, longevity's therapeutics and drug discovery sector pulled $2.1B in VC funding, with autoimmune disease emerging as a core part of the longevity story.

title: Nature Biomarker Study: Data from 700 People Identifies Longevity Markers
description: Data from almost 700 people show that biomarkers that change at a slower pace are markers of longevity, providing new tools for measuring biological aging.

title: Shingles Vaccine Cuts Risk of Serious Cardiac Events by 46% in Large Study
description: In 123,411 patients, shingles vaccination was linked to 46% lower major adverse cardiac events, 66% lower all-cause mortality, and 32% lower heart attack risk, with effects lasting up to 8 years.

title: Myostatin Gene Variants: Humans with MSTN Loss-of-Function Show 10%+ Muscle Mass Increase
description: UK Biobank data from 1.1M individuals shows MSTN loss-of-function carriers have increased lean mass, grip strength, and decreased adiposity, supporting myostatin blockade safety for muscle growth.

title: Rhonda Patrick: 'Is Oxytocin the Missing Link between Social Connection and Longevity?'
description: Rhonda Patrick explores the science linking oxytocin, social connection, and longevity outcomes in her latest FoundMyFitness newsletter.

title: Lifespan.io: Mitochondria Delivery Method Rescues Parkinson's in Mice
description: Scientists used red blood cells to encapsulate healthy mitochondria and deliver them into diseased cells, achieving improvements across multiple Parkinson's disease models.

title: Buck Institute Launches Healthspan Horizons Initiative
description: The Buck Institute for Research on Aging launched Healthspan Horizons, a new initiative to address the challenge of extending healthy years of life through translational aging research.

title: Longevity Center Europe: Clients Aging 22% Slower Than Average (DunedinPACE 0.78)
description: DunedinPACE results from 80+ clients show average biological aging pace of 0.78, with at least 10 clients achieving a pace below Bryan Johnson's reported 0.65.
